title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
summary: Flash is a deployment framework from RunPod that simplifies GPU cloud deployment from an IDE using a single decorator on Python functions. It enables hot reload for rapid iteration, allowing model swaps with one line of code instead of container rebuilds.
writeup: Audry Hsu of RunPod presents Flash, a deployment framework that replaces traditional Docker and cloud allocation workflows with a single decorator on async Python functions. The talk covers hot reload for model swaps and GPU cloud deployment driven directly from an IDE, removing the usual container-and-allocation steps between code and a running model. RunPod supplies the GPU instances, priced at $0.00116 per second on H100s. The framing also touches edge deployment, serverless inference, and model compression.
